2605.02010.title: Reliable AI Needs to Externalize Implicit Knowledge: A Human-AI Collaboration Perspective
2605.02010.paper_summary: This position paper argues that reliable AI requires infrastructure for human validation of implicit knowledge. AI learns from both explicit knowledge (papers, documentation, structured databases) and implicit knowledge (reasoning patterns, debugging processes, intermediate steps). Implicit knowledge remains unexternalized because documentation cost exceeds perceived value -- yet AI learns from it indiscriminately, acquiring both beneficial patterns and harmful biases. Current reliability methods can only verify explicit knowledge against sources, creating a fundamental gap: the most valuable AI capabilities (reasoning, judgment, intuition) are precisely those we cannot verify. We propose Knowledge Objects (KOs) -- structured artifacts that externalize implicit knowledge into forms humans can inspect, verify, and endorse. KOs transform verification economics: what was previously too costly to verify becomes feasible, enabling accumulated human validation to improve reliability over time.
2605.02010.paper_summary_zh: 這份立場文件主張，可靠的人工智慧需要基礎設施來進行人類對隱性知識的驗證。人工智慧從顯性知識（論文、文件、結構化數據庫）和隱性知識（推理模式、除錯過程、中間步驟）中學習。隱性知識仍然未被外部化，因為文件化的成本超過了感知的價值——然而，人工智慧卻不加區別地從中學習，獲取有益的模式和有害的偏見。目前的可靠性方法只能將顯性知識與來源進行驗證，這造成了一個根本性的差距：最有價值的人工智慧能力（推理、判斷、直覺）恰恰是我們無法驗證的。 我們提出知識物件（KOs）——將隱性知識外部化為人類可以檢查、驗證和認可的形式的結構化工件。KOs 轉變了驗證經濟學：以前過於昂貴的驗證變得可行，使得積累的人類驗證能隨著時間的推移提高可靠性。
2605.02010.author: Hengyu Liu et.al.
2605.02010.authors: Hengyu Liu, Tianyi Li, Zhihong Cui, Yushuai Li, Zhangkai Wu, Torben Bach Pedersen, Kristian Torp, Christian S. Jensen
